exemplar.title: Is the national ranking of a university from either the United States or the United Kingdom dependent on the amount of external endowment and funding?

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear sectioning with introduction, methodology, analysis, and conclusion
Comprehensive table of contents and consistent headings enhance navigation
Logical flow of ideas from data collection through analysis to conclusion
weaknesses: Occasional digressions and excessive raw-data tables reduce conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates clear coherence and logical development with sound organization (table of contents, labelled sections), placing it firmly in band 3. However, redundant material (full raw-data tables, repeated definitions) prevents a level-4 score for conciseness and relevance.

strengths: Effective use of multiple representations (tables, boxplots, scatterplots, ranked-difference tables)
Appropriate use of key statistical symbols and labels in many instances
weaknesses: Inconsistent notation (mix of bold, italics, typos like âIORâ instead of âIQRâ)
Errors in sign interpretation and formula application disrupt clarity
Lack of uniform precision in reporting statistics
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the submission makes good use of various representations (band 3), inconsistencies in notation, typographical errors, and occasional unclear commentary limit the work to band 2 (âsome appropriate mathematical languageâ and âmostly clear with some consistencyâ).

strengths: Choice of variables driven by personal university selection interests
Designing a custom sampling method and employing Spearmanâs coefficient shows initiative
weaknesses: Mathematical commentary largely follows standard textbook procedures
Limited evidence of novel data manipulation or creative modeling
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: There is clear and significant personal engagement: the topic is motivated by the studentâs own interests, and independent thinking is evident in data set selection and method. However, creativity and initiative remain moderate, placing the work at band 2 overall.

strengths: Acknowledges methodological limitations and suggests basic extensions
Some reflection on sampling bias and outlier effects
weaknesses: Critical analysis remains descriptive rather than deeply evaluative
Connection between mathematical procedures and conceptual understanding is limited
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reflection goes beyond token comments, offering meaningful insights (band 2), but the critique is basic and the link to deeper understanding is weak, fitting band 1 overall.

strengths: Relevant use of course-level mathematics (descriptive stats, boxplots, Spearmanâs rank)
Correct application of basic statistical tools in parts
Evidence of planning analytical procedures with formula inclusion
weaknesses: Major errors in Spearmanâs calculation and interpretation
Incorrect threshold criteria and sign interpretation for correlations
Omissions in calculation steps (e.g., dividing sums)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration applies appropriate SL syllabus mathematics (band 3), but the presence of serious computational errors and misinterpretations prevents a higher mark. The work demonstrates relevant course-level use but lacks full accuracy and rigor.

strandSelections.reasoning: Selecting two national data sets, devising a sampling method, and choosing Spearmanâs coefficient show initiative beyond a template exercise.  However, the mathematics and commentary largely follow textbook procedures, so evidence of independent thinking is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstanding.â

The student demonstrates understanding of fundamental statistical ideasâcentral tendency, spread, positive skew, and monotonic correlationâbut misinterprets the direction of correlation and overlooks calculation inconsistencies, showing âsome understandingâ rather than thorough comprehension.

Basic calculations of mean, median, quartiles, and scatterplots are correct, yet serious errors occur in the Spearman computations (UK coefficient beyond Â±1 and conflicting sign interpretation).  Consequently, the work is only âpartially correct,â warranting the 2-mark band.

Most material is relevant to answering the research question, yet the report contains sizeable raw-data tables copied in full and repeated explanations of basic statistical terms.  Extended narrative comments (e.g., university choice motivations) introduce redundancy, so the work is only âmostly relevant,â not âhighly concise.â

Key statistical symbols (\(\bar x, r_s, H_0, H_1\)) and units are used, but notation is sometimes inconsistent (mix of bold, italics, and plain text; âr-valueâ vs âRÂ²â) and subscripts are occasionally omitted.  Errors such as âIORâ instead of âIQRâ further prevent full consistency, placing the work in the âsome appropriateâ rather than âmostly appropriateâ band.

Explanations usually accompany figures, but inconsistencies in sign interpretation (positive scatter yet negative correlation commentary) and mis-stated formula results disrupt clarity.  Overall communication is understandable yet not consistently accurate, aligning with the âmostly clear with some consistencyâ descriptor.

Descriptive statistics, boxplots, scatterplots with lines of best fit, and Spearmanâs rank coefficient are all part of the AI SL syllabus and are applied appropriately to the research question.  The mathematics is therefore ârelevant and at course level,â but not sophisticated enough for the higher bands that require advanced or rigorous techniques.

The student provides multiple relevant representations: extensive tables of raw data, two individual box-and-whisker plots, a combined comparative boxplot, two scatterplots (with regression lines), and ranked-difference tables for Spearmanâs calculation.  Each diagram is referenced in the text and helps the reader understand the analysis, meeting the criteria for âeffective useâ though not reaching the sophistication expected for the top band.

The student reflects on methodological limitations (sample size, currency fluctuations, public vs private institutions) and proposes extensions (per-student spending).  These comments move beyond a token reflection, giving âmeaningful reflection,â but do not probe deeply into mathematical validity or alternative statistical models, so they do not reach the highest band.

Beyond the basic decision to compare two countries, the statistical techniques and presentation choices are conventional.  There is limited evidence of novel data manipulation, creative modelling, or inventive visualisation, so only âsome creativity or initiativeâ is apparent.

The exploration is divided into clearly labelled sections (introduction, methodology, descriptive statistics, inferential statistics, conclusion).  Ideas are developed in a logical order: data collection is followed by descriptive work and then by inferential tests, leading to a conclusion that revisits the hypotheses.  Although occasional digressions occur (e.g., repeated statements of the hypotheses and lengthy quotations from sources), the overall flow remains clear and easy to follow, justifying placement in the âcoherent and clear logical developmentâ band rather than the lower âsome coherenceâ band.

Critical analysis is present in a rudimentary formâe.g., acknowledging outliers and sampling biasâbut remains descriptive rather than evaluative.  The student rarely questions the robustness of calculations (e.g., incorrect Spearman result for UK data is not noticed), indicating only âbasic analysis with limited critique.â

While the conclusion reiterates that higher endowment correlates with ranking and links this to the studentâs university choice, the discussion does not convincingly demonstrate how the mathematics deepens the studentâs conceptual understanding of correlation or ranking metrics.  Thus, connection is âlimitedâ rather than âclear.â

A comprehensive table of contents, consistent headings, and page numbers demonstrate sound organisation.  The reader can move from raw data to analysis without difficulty, and the introduction and conclusion frame the body effectively.  Transitions between sections are present but occasionally abrupt; however, the piece still meets the description of being âwell organised with clear introduction, body and conclusion.â

The investigation is clearly motivated by the studentâs own university-selection interests and this personal angle drives the choice of variables, discussion, and conclusion.  The approach is therefore âclear and personal,â yet it does not demonstrably âenhance understandingâ in a notably original way.

annotations.comment: The title clearly conveys the investigationâs focus, indicating both variables and contexts, aiding reader orientation.
Consider rephrasing the research question to specify âto what extentâ endowment influences ranking. This clarifies the relationship under study.
The comprehensive table of contents provides an effective roadmap with clear headings and page references, enhancing navigation.
The introduction effectively contextualizes the investigation through personal rationale, demonstrating clear personal engagement.
The aim restates the research question without specifying the statistical approach. Consider adding reference to Spearmanâs correlation for focus.
To strengthen reproducibility, include details on how endowment and ranking data were aligned year-to-year and any data cleaning steps.
The description of random selection lacks detail on how ties or stratification were handled. Clarify selection procedure to ensure sample representativeness.
Provide a brief caption explaining how the spinning wheel selection was executed to improve clarity of the sampling method.
Credible governmental data sources are appropriately cited, enhancing the investigationâs data reliability.
The outline of descriptive and inferential statistical methods shows coherent planning of analytical procedures.
Notation for hypotheses should use consistent subscript formatting, e.g., \(H_0\) and \(H_1\). Ensure uniform style to avoid confusion.
Clearly labeled raw data section helps the reader locate the original data used for analysis, supporting transparency.
The raw data table is excessively long. Condense by summarizing key values or moving full data to an appendix to improve conciseness.
Specify the precision (e.g., significant figures) consistently when reporting descriptive statistics to maintain accuracy.
The example calculation shows a sum but omits dividing by 5; include the division step in the table to avoid misunderstanding.
Typographical error âIORâ should read âIQR.â Correct the acronym to maintain accurate mathematical communication.
Add axis labels and units to the boxplot image to make the visualization fully interpretable without external reference.
The description interprets median proximity to minimum as positive skew; could you clarify this interpretation by referencing quartile positions explicitly?
Including the Spearman formula and source enhances mathematical rigor and acknowledges academic referencing.
The calculated Spearmanâs coefficient of -0.6369 lies within [-1,1], but the interpretation thresholds â-0.4 > r_s â¥ 0.8â are incorrect; revise strength criteria.
The narrative effectively compares UK and US funding levels, demonstrating the studentâs understanding of contextual differences.
The correlation is described as positive, but since lower ranking numbers indicate better performance, the axis inversion implies a negative relationship; address sign interpretation.
Use consistent notation: the r-value for Spearman should be labeled \(r_s\) rather than an unqualified r to distinguish from Pearsonâs coefficient.
Citation formatting is inconsistent; adopt a single style (e.g., APA or MLA) and apply uniform formatting to all references.
For list items, ensure complete bibliographic information including access dates and DOIs to enhance reference validity.
The result \(r_s = -5.14615\) is mathematically impossible for a correlation coefficient; re-examine and correct the formula application.
Enhance critical analysis by discussing why UK and US correlations differ in magnitude and potential underlying factors.
Extend reflection by considering additional statistical assumptions (e.g., monotonicity, independence) and their impact on results.
